steps: Dissect the third instar larvae in 0 mM Ca 2+ HL-3 at room temperature.
Incubate in 2 mM Ca 2+ /5 mM K + /7 mM glutamate solution for 5 min. 00:05:00 Glutamate treatment desensitizes postsynaptic glutamate receptors, thus reducing muscle contraction upon stimulation.
Measure GCaMP6f fluorescence to indicate the resting Ca 2+ levels.

To image GCaMP6f in high K + stimulations, stimulate larval fillets subsequently with 90 mM K + /2 mM Ca 2+ /7 mM glutamate solution for 10 minutes. 00:10:00 High K + and Ca 2+ lead to bulk Ca 2+ influxes into the muscles and cause dramatic contractions.
After 10-min stimulation, rinse larval fillets with 2 mM Ca 2+ /5 mM K + /7 mM glutamate solution.

Similarly, focus manually the boutons subjected to 1-min 90 mM K + /0.5 mM Ca 2+ /7 mM glutamate stimulation and image at the 30 th to 60 th s every one second. All images were captured from the muscles 6 and 7 of the abdominal segment 3. Each larva was only used for one recording. The images of clearly focused boutons were further used for data quantifications.
Count the GCaMP6f fluorescence intensity in type Ib boutons and surrounding muscles (served as the fluorescence background).
The GCaMP6f fluorescence intensity of at least 10 type Ib boutons from the same muscles 6 and 7 at given time period was averaged to obtain each data value.
Dissect the third instar larvae in 0 mM Ca 2+ HL-3 at room temperature.
Incubate in 2 mM Ca 2+ /5 mM K + /7 mM glutamate solution for 5 min. 00:05:00

Aspirate larval axonal bundle and deliver with 10-40 Hz stimulations via a glass capillary electrode. The stimulus was fixed at 5 mV and 0.5 ms duration by pClamp 10.6 software (Axon Instruments Inc). The images were captured every 2 s using MetaMorph software and ANDOR iXon3 897 camera. All images were captured from the muscles 6 and 7 of abdominal segment 3. Each larva was only used for one recording.
Count the GCaMP6f fluorescence intensity in type Ib boutons and surrounding muscles (served as the fluorescence background).

Calculate final GCaMP6f fluorescence intensity by subtracting background fluorescence intensity in surrounding muscles from GCaMP6f fluorescence intensity in boutons. The GCaMP6f fluorescence intensity of at least 5 type Ib boutons from the same muscles 6 and 7 at given time period was averaged to obtain each data value. Images processing was achieved using Image J and LSM Zen.
Manually focus the boutons and simultaneously image in the 6 th and 10 th min every one second.
creator: Chi-Kuang Yao
